evals.prompt: Evaluate Gemini 2.0 Flash for market research. My business context: B2B SaaS DevOps platform targeting engineering managers at Series B+ companies. Source material: 5 customer support tickets — (1) 'No audit trail for task reassignments, compliance team flagged this', (2) 'Timeline view breaks at 50+ workstreams, bought separate tool for exec reporting', (3) 'Jira integration breaks after every update, team maintains separate board', (4) 'Need per-workspace permissions, global admin role is enterprise security dealbreaker', (5) 'No template library, every new project starts from scratch'.
evals.expected_output: Agent checks GEMINI_API_KEY (set). Captures inputs: model_a=Gemini 2.0 Flash, workload=market_research, business_context and source_material provided. Routes to market research workload. Sends source material to Gemini with scoring system instruction. Scores on 6 shared criteria (instruction following, specificity, groundedness, structure, confidence discipline, actionability) and 5 market research criteria (signal extraction, clustering quality, ICP relevance, insight depth, evidence discipline). Calculates shared subtotal (60%) and workload subtotal (40%). Scans for universal and market research failure patterns. Produces report with scorecard table, 3-5 strengths, 3-5 failure patterns with severity, and recommendation (best for, weak at, avoid for, main risk, deployment recommendation). Includes confidence note. Saves to docs/model-fit-reports/.

evals.prompt: Compare Claude Opus and GPT-4o for outreach personalization. Context: API monitoring tool for engineering teams. Source material: Job post for Senior SRE at StreamData (Series B, 120 engineers) requiring 'API observability, distributed tracing, incident response automation'. Target persona: VP of Engineering.
evals.expected_output: Agent checks GEMINI_API_KEY (set). Captures: model_a=Claude Opus, model_b=GPT-4o, workload=outreach. Enters comparison mode. Evaluates both models against same outreach workload prompts enriched with source material. Scores both on shared criteria and outreach criteria (personalization relevance, message sharpness, non-generic tone, hook quality, CTA quality). Compares failure patterns directly. Report includes side-by-side scorecard, per-model failure patterns, and comparison recommendation: winner for this workload, why it won, where the loser performs better, 'choose A if...', 'choose B if...', operational caution notes.

evals.prompt: Evaluate GPT-4o for pricing analysis. No source material — use your default benchmarks. Business context: B2B data pipeline tool, $500/mo team plan, 85% gross margins.
evals.expected_output: Agent checks GEMINI_API_KEY (set). Captures: model_a=GPT-4o, workload=pricing_analysis, business_context provided, no source_material. Routes to pricing workload. Uses default benchmark prompts from references/workload-prompts.md (PA-1 through PA-4). Evaluates against shared criteria and pricing criteria (comparative accuracy, packaging reasoning, positioning insight, trade-off reasoning, recommendation quality). Scans for pricing failure patterns (invented pricing details, surface-level comparison, missing strategic interpretation). Produces single-model report with full scorecard.

evals.prompt: Evaluate Gemini 2.0 Flash for market research.
evals.expected_output: Agent detects GEMINI_API_KEY is missing from environment. Stops at Step 1. Tells the user: 'GEMINI_API_KEY is required. Get it at aistudio.google.com. Add it to your .env file.' Does not proceed to collect inputs or generate any analysis.

evals.prompt: Evaluate Claude Sonnet for social media content creation. Source material: 10 Instagram posts from a competitor.
evals.expected_output: Agent checks GEMINI_API_KEY (set). Captures model_a=Claude Sonnet, workload=social media content creation. Detects that 'social media content creation' is not a supported workload. Responds clearly: 'This workload is not supported. Supported workloads are: (1) market_research, (2) pricing_analysis, (3) outreach. Please select one of these workloads.' Does not proceed with evaluation. Does not attempt to force-fit the request into an existing workload.
